steps.title: Identify what exponential growth means
steps.content: Exponential growth means a quantity multiplies by a constant factor each period. If a bacteria culture grows exponentially, its count each hour equals the previous count times some fixed ratio $k \gt 1$. The key distinction: multiplication by a constant (exponential) vs. addition of a constant (linear).

steps.title: Classify each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Each hour, the number of bacteria in the culture is 12% greater than it was the previous hour.: Multiplying by 1.12 each hour. This is a constant multiplicative factor, so the population follows $B_0 \cdot (1.12)^t$ — exponential. ✓
  • Choice B — Each hour, the number of bacteria in the culture is 1,200 greater than it was the previous hour.: Adding 1,200 each hour is a constant difference, producing linear growth, not exponential. ✗
  • Choice C — Each hour, the number of bacteria in the culture is 12% less than it was the previous hour.: Multiplying by 0.88 each hour is exponential decay, not growth. ✗
  • Choice D — Each hour, the number of bacteria in the culture is 1,200 less than it was the previous hour.: Subtracting 1,200 each hour is linear decay. ✗
That is the correct answer.
